title: Neoporteria crispa
taxonomy: Neoporteria crispa is classified within the family Cactaceae, commonly known as cacti. This classification places it within the subfamily Opuntioideae, a group of cacti characterized by their flat stems and rounded areoles. The genus Neoporteria itself is a part of the tribe Eulychiae, a group of New World cacti. The species Neoporteria crispa is native to South America, where it grows in arid and semi-arid regions. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the diverse family of cacti.

intro: Neoporteria crispa, also known as Copiapoa crispa, is a species of cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae, native to the Atacama Desert in Chile. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Neoporteria crispa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive problems.
